layers: L0: Brand Bible (brand-bible.json) - Single Source of Truth
L1: Studio Integration Schema (studio-integration-schema.json) - UI Contract
L2: Generation Pipeline - Prompt assembly + model dispatch
L3: Validation Pipeline - Post-gen quality gates
L4: Media Hub DAM - Asset management + versioning
L5: Distribution - Social, print, video, in-store
architecture_overview.data_flow: Bible → Studio UI → Prompt Assembly → Model (Flux/SDXL/Video) → Validation → Approved Asset → Media Hub → Distribution Channels

phase_1.name: Brand Bible & Schema Foundation
deliverables: brand-bible.json - Full character definitions, palette, pattern, typography, packaging, scene mapping, prompt anchors, generation rules
studio-integration-schema.json - UI binding contract, prompt assembly pipeline, validation pipeline, video extensions, asset registry
phase_1.what_this_solves: The exact problem shown in the Studio screenshot - wrong character look due to missing structured brand data feeding the prompt pipeline

tasks.task: Wire CHARACTER ANCHOR dropdown to brand-bible.json character entries
tasks.detail: Each selection auto-injects visual_prompt_anchor + do_not rules as negative prompt

tasks.task: Add SCENE CONTEXT dropdown per studio-integration-schema
tasks.detail: Auto-configures palette focus, available characters, mood, and pattern usage
Follows the 6-step order: scene → character → user prompt → palette → plaid → negatives

tasks.task: Add character reference image preview panel
tasks.detail: Shows the asset_ref images alongside the generated image for quick visual comparison

tasks.task: Implement 'Append brand palette & plaid context' checkbox logic
tasks.detail: Auto-detects if selected character needs plaid injection (Mama Zainab, ZuZu)
Extract dominant colors from generated image, compare to brand palette, flag off-brand
Use face embedding model to compare generated faces against reference assets
Object detection for: plaid apron, ZuZu ribbon, packaging pattern, logo presence
Distinguish diamond weave from tartan/gingham/checkered - reject wrong patterns
Take script scenes + brand bible → generate storyboard frames → animate to video
IP-Adapter or ControlNet-based character locking using reference assets
Auto-generate branded overlays using plaid_v2 pattern + Bebas Neue typography
All approved generated assets auto-cataloged with character tags, scene tags, brand compliance score
Pre-sized templates (Instagram 1:1, Story 9:16, FB 16:9, Twitter 2:1) with brand frame overlays
CMYK conversion + bleed guides for packaging, menus, car wraps, kiosk panels
Track brand compliance scores across all generated content over time

immediate_next_steps: 1. Feed brand-bible.json into the Studio backend as the /brand/* API endpoint
2. Update Studio UI CHARACTER ANCHOR dropdown to consume character entries with asset previews
3. Implement prompt assembly pipeline (Phase 2.3) - this alone would have prevented the screenshot failure
4. Add the reference image preview panel so operators can visually verify before distributing
